purpose: Frozen EXTERNAL confirmatory target set for the Egyptian external-anchor channel: Egyptian group-writing renderings (Kom el-Hetan Liste EN, Amenophis III) of Cretan toponyms whose Aegean forms are INDEPENDENTLY known from Linear B. Intended ONLY for a future preregistered one-shot Cretan-anchor test (apply the FROZEN Egyptian->foreign correspondence model to each Egyptian oval; check recovery of the known Linear B skeleton). This set is externally fixed by the ancient list, not chosen to fit anything (invariant 8). FIREWALL: it must NEVER be an input to Linear A hypothesis formation or scoring.

primary_source.ref: Edel, E. (†) & Görg, M. 2005. Die Ortsnamenlisten im nördlichen Säulenhof des Totentempels Amenophis' III. Ägypten und Altes Testament 50. Wiesbaden: Harrassowitz. ISBN 3-447-05219-8.
primary_source.collation: DIRECT, from the primary-edition text + apparatus + Edel's own line-drawings (Fig. 1-4; reconstruction table book p.191).
primary_source.plate_verification_outstanding: Tafel 12 photo too eroded to read ovals at sign level; the Falttafeln 13/14 hand-copy facsimiles are ABSENT from the scanned PDF. Freeze rests on Edel's edition drawings, not on legible photographic plates. Source the fold-out Falttafeln separately for independent sign-level confirmation.

discipline: FREEZE ONLY — no Linear A matching, no sign-value claim, no external preregistration performed in producing this artifact (invariants 3, 8). Any model forecast enters downstream capped ≤0.75 (invariant 5).

targets.certainty: sicher / undisputed (‘Zu Knossos selbst braucht wohl kein Wort verloren zu werden’); ku-nu-š = ‘das Abbild von ko-no-so’
editorial_alternatives: none — Kitchen's ko-no-so accepted without reservation (Ventris–Chadwick, Documents 146–147)

targets.en_row: li 11 (primary); doubled on the Vorderseite at li 1 (palimpsest over erased Amykla)
targets.identification: Amnisos, the harbour of inland Knossos (Strabo)
targets.certainty: sicher / ‘sehr gut’; the li 10/li 11 pair (Knossos/Amnisos) mutually reinforce; Osing on-block collation (1972/73) confirms ‘amniš’
editorial_alternatives: li 1 (Vorderseite) is a genuine palimpsest: Amniša recut over the erased mainland name Amykla (*ʾ-m-k-r/l, older singular of Ἀμύκλαι)
aleph/ayin printed identically (apostrophe) by Edel — leading a-sign (ʾ vs ʿ) not confidently distinguishable at scan resolution; rendered ʾ by convention

targets.certainty: oval reading rkt undisputed; identification per Kitchen (Orientalia 34, 1965, 6 n.1).
editorial_alternatives: i/e↔u vacillation in the first syllable (reed vs LB u) noted as normal in Greek adoption of pre-Greek words
Faure/Kirsten dispute only the LOCATION (inland vs coastal), not the reading

targets.en_row: li 2 (Vorderseite/front, palimpsest — the VISIBLE/recut name); original Cretan-side oval li 14 physically lost
targets.identification: Phaistos (Φαιστος, southern Crete) — the VISIBLE/final name
targets.certainty: visible name sicher/wahrscheinlich (Edel's own reading, corroborated by LB pa-i-to; confirmed by Osing autopsy 1972/73). Erased UNDERTEXT identity (Pisaia vs Pisatis) explicitly fraglich.
editorial_alternatives: PALIMPSEST: the visible Phaistos was recut over an erased mainland name — Πισαια (undertext ending -y) OR Πισατις (ending -tj); Edel cannot decide between the two
original Cretan-side oval li 14 is physically LOST; its [bā-y-šā-tj] is a reconstruction from the palimpsest-source theory
Faure (Kadmos 7, 1968) & Sergent read Bi-ša-ja = Πισαια (Peloponnese) for the whole oval — Edel rejects (drops the y and the tj)
Helck expected a place near Rhethymnon (Itinerarhypothese) — Görg rejects Helck, upholds Edel's Phaistos; earlier doubts ‘sans vérification sur le bloc’ overturned by Osing's on-the-block autopsy

targets.en_row: li 3 (Vorderseite/front, palimpsest — the VISIBLE/recut name); original Cretan-side oval li 15 physically lost
targets.certainty: sicher for the visible name (‘entspricht verblüffend genau dem Stadtnamen Kydonia’); vocalization ku-tu-n(i)-ja makes it ‘noch perfekter’
editorial_alternatives: PALIMPSEST: recut over an erased primary Edel left UNIDENTIFIED (≈5 signs; begins jj/y or ʿa, ends r/l). (MG:) Görg proposes li 3* = a second citation of Amyklai (book p.203), disputing Edel's abstention
original Cretan-side oval li 15 physically LOST; its [Kā-tw-nā-y] is a reconstruction

secondary_robustness_anchor.identification: Kythera (the island BETWEEN Crete and the Peloponnese)
secondary_robustness_anchor.note: Fully freezable in its own right, but structurally the bridge island separating the two 7-name blocks — NOT a strictly-Cretan toponym; use only as a robustness anchor.

excluded.reason: phonetic reading firm (w-i-r/l-i-ja) but GEOGRAPHIC identity contested (Ilios/Troy vs Elis vs Elea/NW-Crete vs Helos/Laconia); Görg 'Eleia?' fraglich; no secure LB comparandum
only traces survive; no continuous transliteration recoverable; Görg '[S]it[eia]?' (restored + fraglich)
physically lost / bracketed reconstructions; the freezable readings come from the surviving Vorderseite palimpsests li 2 / li 3 — listing these would double-count a conjectural oval
mainland Peloponnesian name, only the erased undertext beneath the visible Phaistos — not a Cretan target

excluded.item: Mainland/Peloponnese block + Leitnamen (li 1* Amykla, li 4 Mykene, li 5 Thebais/Tegea?, li 6 Messene, li 7 Nauplia; heading re 1 Keftiu/Kreta, re 2 Tanaja/Danaia)
excluded.reason: not Cretan toponyms — Peloponnesian/mainland entries + the two list-heading Leitnamen
